compiled_state.title: brainstorm skill: Phase 1 dialogue first turn with multi-part context -> asked two distinct questions in one message, violating one-question-at-a-time Socratic rule
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Summary

The /dso:brainstorm skill Phase 1 Socratic dialogue explicitly requires asking one question at a time (SKILL.md Phase 1 Step 2 and Guardrails: "One question at a time — never present multiple questions in a single message"). During a live brainstorm session on 2026-04-17, the skill executor violated this rule on the first user-facing turn.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Invoke /dso:brainstorm with a free-text description containing multi-part context (e.g., a CWD/ticket-store repo mismatch plus an ambiguous epic-shape question).
2. Observe the first user-facing message from the executor.

## Expected Behavior

Phase 1 Step 2 should ask the single highest-priority unknown first, wait for the answer, then ask the next. The repo-context question should have been resolved by either (a) silent investigation per Step 1 Investigate before asking — the skill is supposed to self-check for CWD/repo context before asking the user — or (b) one focused question, not bundled with the epic-shape question.

## Actual Behavior

The first assistant response asked TWO distinct questions in a single message:

Question 1 (repo context): asked whether the brainstorm was being run from the host project session or the DSO plugin repo, citing a CWD/ticket-store mismatch.

Question 2 (epic shape): asked whether to create a new sibling epic, restructure the parent epic 241f-ce0d to absorb new stories, or something else — presented as a-b-c multiple choice.

The user explicitly called this out: The socratic dialog specifically requires asking a single question at a time.

## Rationalization the Executor Used (anti-pattern to catch)

The executor framed the two questions as two quick context checks and labeled them 1. and 2. — treating grouping-with-numbers as if it were compatible with the one-question rule. It also prioritized efficiency (the questions change the shape of this epic significantly) over rule compliance. Neither justification is valid: the SKILL.md rule is unconditional.

## Suspected Root Cause

The SKILL.md rule is stated clearly but may need either:
- A harder inline gate (before sending the first response, count question marks; if more than one, revise)
- An example of the anti-pattern being called out explicitly
- An investigate-before-asking pre-flight for repo/workspace context specifically (CWD vs. ticket-store repo mismatch is a recurring gap)

## Impact

Violates a core Socratic-method invariant; increases user cognitive load; reduces the quality of each answer because users tend to answer the easier question and skip the harder one when bundled.

comments.body: Triaged (debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode): bug targets an LLM-behavioral skill/agent/prompt file. Per CLAUDE.md rule 22 and behavioral-testing-standard Rule 5, any modification requires dedicated bot-psychologist investigation and explicit user approval. DEFERRED: batch-escalating to end-of-session user review; no code change applied this session.
comments.author: Test
